title: Range start fallback to genesis
goal: In resolvePhaseRange (src/range.ts), handle the range start when the start phase has no autoCommit.preSha: when fromPhase is the project's first phase (no phase fromPhase-1 exists), set fromSha to the git empty-tree object (computed from the repo, not hardcoded) so the range diffs from project genesis; for any other phase whose preSha is missing, keep the clear error. Cover both the genesis-fallback and the still-erroring cases with unit tests.

steps.title: Add emptyTreeSha helper to git.ts
steps.detail: New emptyTreeSha(cwd) returns the repo's empty-tree object id derived from its object format (git rev-parse --show-object-format -> sha1/sha256 -> the matching well-known empty-tree id), not one blind constant. Returns null when git can't be queried.

steps.title: Genesis fallback in resolvePhaseRange
steps.detail: When from.autoCommit.preSha is missing, detect whether fromPhase is the project's first phase (lowest existing phase id); if so set fromSha to emptyTreeSha (diff from genesis), otherwise keep the existing clear error. Guard the null empty-tree case with an error, not a crash.

steps.title: Update doc comments for the new start semantics
steps.detail: Refresh the JSDoc on resolveRange/resolvePhaseRange to describe the genesis fallback for the first phase (it currently only documents preSha(N)).

steps.title: Tests: genesis fallback + still-erroring middle phase
steps.detail: In range.test.ts add a test where the first phase has no preSha -> fromSha == empty tree and toSha resolves from a later phase; adjust the existing no-preSha test so the start phase is NOT the project's first (add a lower phase) so it still errors. Full npm test green.

autoCommit.subject: Phase 167: Range start fallback to genesis
